design_read: A grip-first baseball craft system where the first useful object is the hand on the ball, not a stat panel.
audience: Visitors, players, coaches, and parents trying to understand pitch grips before reading measured movement.
surface_type: Mixed editorial product and interactive learning app.
primary_job: Show how to hold and release a pitch before exposing spin, break, and source ledgers.
content_grain: Interactive 3D object, sourced prose, grip controls, release sequence, and provenance-backed numbers.
keep_override_reasons: Kept the raw pamphlet and craft-room posture, but grounded it in the existing parchment and leather Baseball Atlas tokens. The 3D depth role was raised because hand, thumb, seam, and palm gap carry comprehension.
signature_move: A procedural hand-ball grip theater appears in the first viewport and remains the state source for the Grip Lab controls.
layout_architecture: Dark first-viewport grip theater followed by family rail, dark grip workspace, release room, movement translation, and evidence ledger.
information_flow: Grip first, release second, movement third, evidence last.
section_or_zone_rhythm: Large dark craft rooms alternate with lighter reading and ledger rooms.
primary_visual_asset: Original procedural baseball, seam curve, stitches, finger pads, palm, and thumb geometry.
asset_job: Teach contact, pressure, ball depth, thumb support, and release feel.
source_route: Local pitch records and private PDF reference, no copied PDF images or prose.
prompt_or_query: Baseball Atlas Pitch Atlas grip-first baseball craft app with hand ball seams thumb finger pads release feel, dark serious but fast usable frontend.
license_usage_state: Original code and geometry. PDF used as private reference only.
fallback_if_unavailable: No-WebGL SeamSchematic uses the same seam anchors and grip contacts.
two_d_three_d_blend_strategy: Semantic DOM owns text and controls; the 3D scene owns grip comprehension; the 2D schematic is the fallback twin.
motion_grammar: Low idle GPU. Static study views by default; OrbitControls only on user inspection.
interaction_model: Pitch selection, top side thumb view controls, right left hand toggle, grip overlay toggle, and sourced details disclosure.
token_source: Baseball materials: aged leather, parchment, red seams, dark bullpen stage, bone text.
forbidden_repeats: No stat-first dashboard, no generic centered hero, no decorative 3D preview box, no copied PDF assets.
state_model: Loading via Suspense schematic, error via SpecimenBoundary schematic, empty community state, populated pitch records, source as-of date from registry.
performance_budget: Lazy Three scene, demand frameloop, no idle spin in study scenes, no external texture fetches, static standalone prototype.
recent_fingerprints: Prior build used a pitch specimen and source ledger but did not make the hand and grip theater the first interaction.
repeat_exemption: Existing repo tokens are retained because they already fit the baseball material system.
divergence_note: The design changes the first viewport and page order from specimen plus source archive into grip theater plus craft path.
restraint_clause: No extra data widgets above grip learning; measured movement stays below Release Room.
